{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"**Triage Order**

1. `src/utils.ts` — refactor first, but incrementally.
   This is the biggest blast-radius reducer: `fan-in 18`, `fan-out 1`, `blast_radius 0.58`, plus a `large_file` finding: `424 non-empty lines`, `33 top-level functions`. Because so many files import it, I would not do a broad rewrite. First split stable, low-coupling helper clusters into focused modules while keeping compatibility exports from `utils.ts`.

2. `src/plugin.ts` — second priority.
   It has high inbound dependency pressure: `fan-in 10`, `fan-out 3`, `blast_radius 0.58`, and a large method: `buildFinished`, `103 lines`, `1.7x` threshold. Since it is load-bearing and test gap is `1`, start by extracting internal helpers from `buildFinished` without changing the public plugin surface.

3. `src/esbuild/index.ts` — third priority.
   It combines high outbound complexity with a very large function: `fan-in 1`, `fan-out 11`, and `runEsbuild` is `237 lines`, `4.0x` threshold. This file is a coordination hub more than a shared API, so refactoring can reduce local complexity without as much importer churn. Extract phases from `runEsbuild` first.

4. `src/index.ts` — fourth, carefully.
   It has the highest fan-out: `fan-in 4`, `fan-out 17`, plus the main `large_file` finding: `459 non-empty lines`, `28 top-level functions`. It also contains several large nested functions: `build` at `293 lines`, anonymous callback at `274`, `mainTasks` at `194`, `normalizeOptions` at `96`. I would split this only after tests are stable, because it likely coordinates the package’s main behavior.

5. `src/api-extractor.ts` / `src/tsc.ts` / `src/rollup.ts` — opportunistic cleanup.
   `src/api-extractor.ts` has `fan-out 6`; `src/rollup.ts` has `fan-out 7`; both also participate in duplicate `getDuration` logic with `src/tsc.ts`. These are good secondary wins: extract the shared duration helper and clock handling, but don’t treat them as top blast-radius refactors.

**Leave Alone For Now**

`src/log.ts` has `fan-in 9`, `fan-out 0`, but no large-file or large-function finding. It is load-bearing but probably small and stable, so avoid churn unless changing logging behavior.

`test/utils.ts` has `fan-in 9`, `fan-out 0`, but lower `agent_risk 0.34` and test-only blast radius. Leave it until production hotspots are under control.

Small plugin files like `src/plugins/swc-target.ts`, `src/plugins/terser.ts`, and `src/rollup/ts-resolve.ts` have large-function findings around the threshold, but their connectivity is not as compelling. Refactor them only when touching those features anyway."}}
